markdown:
#  Deactivate Funding Accounts as a Payor 
#####  General Availability in Select Markets (United States)
![Flag Us](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/eb8f21ce49403a47d7bb5a0cedc7e0e9/7c0ec0977e654cc6bc9f3d8efa80fbe3/flag-us.png?fm=webp&h=14) [Procore Pay ](https://www.procore.com/pay) is available in the United States. It is designed for General Contractors and Owner-Builders who act as their own General Contractors on a job. Procore Pay extends the [Invoice Management](https://www.procore.com/invoice-management) functionality in the Procore web application to handle the payment process between general and specialty contractors.
##  Background 
Only a [Payments Admin](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/payor-setup-guide/authorize-payment-admins) can deactivate a funding account in the Payor environment. You will deactivate accounts when you no longer want your Payments Disburser to select that account for Drawdown requests on a new Disbursement.
##  Things to Consider 
  * [Required User Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/permissions)


##  Prerequisites 
  * [Add Funding Accounts](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/about-the-payment-processing-tab-in-the-payments-tool/add-funding-accounts)


##  Steps 
  1. Navigate to the Company level **Payments** tool.
  2. Click the Payments Settings ![Bqxdpwowtfshtk Wbduisqkugqf 683kivyxdsblqahu2ajcdpuzzukjg6nfxkqrpmbtxyc9on9ir5fa3zwizng7izardthz B6tvegjyypnzbpqae6skxqu5xxd2xn88qcnq3qjheysyr9 8sxiip0](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/46df908e88adbe5561987f4eb931353a/bba70d51a6ce47599600f0d4f600f10f/bqXDpwowTfShtk-WbDUisQkUgqF_683KiVyxDSblqAHu2ajcDPuzZUkjG6nFXkqrPMbTxyc9on9IR5FA3ZWIZNg7IzaRdThz-B6TvegjyyPNZBPQaE6skxqU5xXD2Xn8?fm=webp&h=15) icon.  
This opens the Payments Settings page. The 'Business Entities' page in the 'Payments Processing' tab is active by default. This page lists each Business Entity configured to pay invoices in your company's Procore Pay software.
  3. Locate the business entity to modify and click its funding account(s) link.  
![Funding Account Link](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/c3e91c1c77f780cc9e083b499b4fcfc4/5c8d6a36ef3310ef98a587256e89486c/funding-account-link.png?fm=webp)
  4. Locate the funding account to edit.
  5. Click the **Overflow** menu and choose **Deactivate** from the drop-down menu. 
#####  Tip
**Can I deactivate a default account?** No. A default account cannot be deactivated unless you first designate another account as the default. See [Set a Default Funding Account as a Payor](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/about-the-payment-processing-tab-in-the-payments-tool/set-a-default-funding-account).
![Deactivate Funding Account](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/3a26fdf50cbc0333342803d5c3bbd1be/eaaca6033663dbfb1c874439b3ec9e25/deactivate-funding-account.png?fm=webp)  

  6. In the 'Deactivate Funding Account' confirmation prompt, review the message. 
#####  Important
Before you deactivate the funding account, be aware of the following:
     * If the account being deactivated is also your default funding account, a Payments Admin must first set a new default account for those projects. See [Update Your Project's Bank Accounts](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/tutorials/manage-payments-project-controls-for-a-single-project).
     * Once deactivated, Payments Disbursers can no longer select the funding account when creating new disbursements.
     * Drawdown requests for existing disbursements are NOT canceled. If the existing disbursement's funds request is successful, Procore Pay withdraws the aggregate disbursement amount from your funding account and transfers it to your deposit account to process invoice payments.
![Deactivate Funding Account Confirm](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/94a43b4a575546dda73926e5cf38ce5c/40cc602aacd3955386851ccbd86af3c3/deactivate-funding-account-confirm.png?fm=webp)
  7. If you agree, click **Deactivate**.   
Procore Pay marks the account _Deactivated.  
  
_![Deactivated Funding Account Marked](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/33e99eef3835126807c63aa838adae91/5682316fe4a7fb23877a47b65ac607f1/deactivated-funding-account-marked.png?fm=webp)


## See Also
  * [View Funding Accounts as a Payor](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/tutorials/view-funding-accounts-as-a-payor)
  * [Set a Default Funding Account as a Payor](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/tutorials/set-a-default-funding-account-as-a-payor)
  * [Configure Payment Processing as a Payor](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/tutorials/configure-payment-processing-as-a-payor)


In Procore Pay, a _payor_ is a general contractor who sends invoice payments to specialty contractors.
In Procore Pay, a _Payments Disburser_ is a Procore user granted permission to create and view disbursements in the Company level Payments tool. Because of the sensitive nature of payments, only a Payments Admin can add/remove disbursers.
In Procore Pay, a _drawdown_ refers to the disbursement of funds from a payor's funding account to the payor's deposit account held by a financial institution. 
  

A _disbursement_ is the action of paying out money from a fund. In Procore Pay, a disbursement withdraws funds from a general contractor's funding account and transmits them to the general contractor's deposit account. Payment orders are then issued to withdraw funds from the deposit account to pay invoices.
In Procore Pay, a business entity is an organization recognized as separate from its owner(s). Some construction companies operate as a single business entity, while others operate as multiple business entities, with each entity, subsidiary, or division focusing on overseeing a different market or sector.
title: Deactivate Funding Accounts as a Payor
description: How to deactivate a funding account for Procore Pay as a Payments Admin.
